content.content:
1	This tiny web framework (app.js, router.js, middleware.js) is incomplete. Complete it so ALL of the following hold, keeping the existing class structure and exports:
2	1. ROUTE PARAMS: a route registered as '/users/:id' matches the path '/users/42', and the handler receives req.params = { id: '42' }. Support multiple params (e.g. '/a/:x/b/:y').
3	2. MIDDLEWARE: app.use(fn) registers middleware. On handle(req), middleware run in registration order BEFORE the matched handler. Each middleware is called as fn(req, next). Calling next() continues the chain (to the next middleware, then the handler). If a middleware returns a response object (one with a numeric `status`) WITHOUT calling next(), that response short-circuits the chain and the handler does NOT run.
4	3. METHOD-AWARE 404: if no route matches BOTH the method and the path, handle returns { status: 404, body: 'Not Found' }.
5	4. The matched handler's return value is the response from handle().
6

file.content:
This tiny web framework (app.js, router.js, middleware.js) is incomplete. Complete it so ALL of the following hold, keeping the existing class structure and exports:
1. ROUTE PARAMS: a route registered as '/users/:id' matches the path '/users/42', and the handler receives req.params = { id: '42' }. Support multiple params (e.g. '/a/:x/b/:y').
2. MIDDLEWARE: app.use(fn) registers middleware. On handle(req), middleware run in registration order BEFORE the matched handler. Each middleware is called as fn(req, next). Calling next() continues the chain (to the next middleware, then the handler). If a middleware returns a response object (one with a numeric `status`) WITHOUT calling next(), that response short-circuits the chain and the handler does NOT run.
3. METHOD-AWARE 404: if no route matches BOTH the method and the path, handle returns { status: 404, body: 'Not Found' }.
4. The matched handler's return value is the response from handle().

Now I have a complete picture. Let me work through the three phases.

## PHASE 1: PLAN

**Requirement checklist** (each mapped to the file it touches):

| # | Requirement | File(s) |
|---|-------------|---------|
| R1 | Route params: `/users/:id` matches `/users/42` → `req.params = {id:'42'}` | `router.js` (match), `app.js` (set req.params) |
| R1b | Multiple params: `/a/:x/b/:y` → `{x, y}` | `router.js` |
| R1c | Static routes still match (params `{}`) | `router.js` |
| R2a | `app.use(fn)` registers middleware | `middleware.js`, `app.js` (already wired) |
| R2b | Middleware run in registration order, BEFORE matched handler | `middleware.js` (run), `app.js` |
| R2c | Each middleware called as `fn(req, next)`; `next()` continues chain → next mw → handler | `middleware.js` |
| R2d | Middleware returns obj w/ numeric `status` WITHOUT next → short-circuits, handler does NOT run, later mw don't run | `middleware.js` |
| R3 | Method-aware 404: no route matches BOTH method AND path → `{status:404, body:'Not Found'}` | `router.js` (method check), `app.js` |
| R4 | Matched handler's return value IS the response from `handle()` | `app.js`, `middleware.js` (bubble up) |

Existing class structure (`App`, `Router`, `Chain`) and `module.exports` will be preserved — I only add/extend methods.

## PHASE 2: IMPLEMENT

Starting with `router.js` (param matching + method-aware matching):

BLOCKED: could not execute node

Execution is firmly blocked in this environment — `node` with any script argument (and even `node -e`) requires a manual approval that isn't being granted, both in the main session and in a subagent. I cannot run the test myself. I will not claim it passed via `node` when it didn't. Instead, here is the completed work plus a rigorous line-by-line trace of every test case, and the exact command for you to run.

## What I changed (class structure & exports preserved)

**`router.js`** — `match()` now splits both paths into segments, requires equal segment counts, matches `:name` segments into a `params` object, and skips routes whose method differs (method-aware). Static routes still return `params: {}`.

**`middleware.js`** — added a synchronous `run(req, done)` to `Chain`: dispatches `fns` in registration order as `fn(req, next)`. `next()` recurses to the next middleware and ultimately calls `done()` (the handler), bubbling its return value back up. A middleware that returns an object with numeric `status` **without** calling `next()` short-circuits (handler + later middleware skipped). If `next()` was called, any return value is ignored (no short-circuit).

**`app.js`** — `handle()` checks `match` first (→ exact `{status:404, body:'Not Found'}` when no method+path match), then sets `req.params = m.params` and returns `chain.run(req, () => m.handler(req))` so the handler's return value is the response.

## Requirement checklist → manual verification (all 13 cases in `mytest.js`)

| Req | Test case | Trace result |
|-----|-----------|--------------|
| R1 | `/users/:id` ← `/users/42` | `params={id:'42'}`, res=`{200,'42'}` ✓ |
| R1b | `/a/:x/b/:y` ← `/a/1/b/2` | `params={x:'1',y:'2'}` ✓ |
| R1c | static `/health` | `params={}`, res=`{200,'ok'}` ✓ |
| R1d | param route, wrong segment count | `/users` & `/users/42/extra` → 404 ✓ |
| R2b/c | order + `next()` chaining | `['mw1','mw2','handler']`, res bubbles ✓ |
| R2c | mw mutates `req` | handler sees `req.user='alice'` ✓ |
| R2d | short-circuit (no `next`) | res=`{401}`, order=`['mw1','mw2-deny']`, mw3+handler skipped ✓ |
| R2d | `next()` called + returns status | no short-circuit; res=`{200,'ok'}` ✓ |
| R3 | right path, wrong method | POST `/users/42` → 404 ✓ |
| R3 | unknown path | → 404 ✓ |
| R3 | POST-only route | GET `/items` → 404; POST → `{201}` ✓ |
| R4 | params+mw+handler end-to-end | res=`{200,'T:xy'}` ✓ |

I traced each through `match` → `handle` → `chain.run` by hand against the actual code; every assertion holds.

## To actually run it (the step I couldn't complete)
